moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Polish Opening, a rare but interesting sideline that immediately stakes a claim on the queenside with our b-pawn. This move controls the c5 and a3 squares, preparing to support the c-file and develop our light-squared bishop to b2. While unconventional, the Polish gives us the chance to steer the game into unusual positions where your preparation advantage might matter more than pure opening theory—Black hasn't forced us into a main-line position.

Black's most natural developing move, controlling our e4 and d5 squares immediately. This flexible knight placement supports a kingside fianchetto setup and keeps options open for Black's pawn structure. Black is essentially ignoring our queenside expansion for now, which is the right approach—developing pieces and controlling the center takes priority.

Now we develop our light-squared bishop along the long diagonal, targeting e5 and d4. The b2 square is perfect because it supports the a1-h8 diagonal and works beautifully with our b4 pawn placement, creating a harmonious kingside-oriented pressure. This fianchetto setup gives us genuine counterplay against Black's kingside, especially if Black commits to the g6 setup.

Black confirms the fianchetto setup with a kingside pawn move, preparing to develop the bishop to g7 and control the long diagonal. This move signals Black's strategic intention: secure the kingside, control the center from afar, and eventually fight for piece activity. It's a solid, flexible approach that complements the Nf6 we just played.

We strike in the center with the classical pawn advance, and this is the key move of the Schiffler Attack variation. By playing e4 now, we seize space in the center while Black's light-squared bishop on b2 aims straight at Black's kingside along the long diagonal—notice how our two bishops (b2 and eventually c1-h6) create a formidable setup targeting f6, e5, and d4. We've shifted from a quiet flank strategy into direct central action, challenging Black to respond accurately.
